title: Implement tempo.embed — walk knowledge/ → knowledge.lance
description: Build src/tempo/embed.py that: (1) walks knowledge/**/*.md, (2) parses YAML frontmatter, (3) chunks body semantically (~500-token windows with overlap), (4) embeds via local fastembed BGE-small (384-dim), (5) writes to data/vectors/knowledge.lance with columns (id, text, vector, path, topic, credibility, source_ids, phase, chunk_idx). Idempotent: re-running on unchanged files is a no-op; changed files replace their rows by path. Unlisted sources get credibility='unvetted' in the row.
acceptance_criteria: tempo.embed.rebuild() writes N rows where N = sum of chunks across knowledge/; re-running produces identical row count; modifying one file and re-running updates only that file's rows; chunks from a doc with no sources list get credibility='unvetted'.

title: Add coach vectors rebuild + coach vectors search CLI verbs
description: Extend Typer app with a 'vectors' subcommand group. 'coach vectors rebuild [--force]' reindexes all of knowledge/. 'coach vectors search QUERY [--topic T] [--credibility-min C] [--k N]' prints ranked snippets with path, credibility tag, and score. Respect the coaching convention: unvetted sources flagged in output.
acceptance_criteria: coach vectors rebuild completes and reports chunks_indexed; coach vectors search 'polarized Z2 duration' returns snippets from polarized-training.md ranked first; --topic nutrition filters correctly.

title: Tests for tempo.embed — idempotency, filters, unvetted flagging
description: pytest coverage: (1) empty index → rebuild → expected row count; (2) rebuild twice → identical rows; (3) modify one file → only that file's rows change; (4) doc with unlisted source → row tagged unvetted; (5) search returns results in expected order; (6) filter by topic excludes off-topic docs. Use a tiny tmp knowledge dir fixture; no network.
acceptance_criteria: uv run pytest tests/test_embed.py -q shows all green; coverage of tempo.embed is meaningful (>=80%).

title: [Phase 1 GAP] Compute aerobic decoupling on activities
description:
Plan's Phase 1 derive.py bullet lists 'decoupling' alongside CTL/ATL/TSB. Current state: activities.decoupling column exists but is always NULL.

Why deferred during initial Phase 1 build:
- ActivitySummary does not include streams (HR/power time-series). Only full Activity (endpoint /activity/<id>) includes streams.
- Computing Pw:HR (or Pa:HR for run) decoupling requires streams for *every* activity synced — O(n) extra API calls per sync. Naive implementation makes sync slow and high-request.

Implementation options:
1. Lazy: add a 'coach derive decoupling' verb that walks activities with decoupling IS NULL and fetches streams one at a time (rate-limited). Cheap to introduce, doesn't slow sync. Most activities only need it computed once.
2. Eager: extend sync.py to optionally fetch streams for activities where icu_training_load > some threshold (e.g. > 60 min endurance sessions). More complex.
3. Use get_activity_streams MCP tool and a dedicated script.

Formula: first-half Pw:HR / second-half Pw:HR, expressed as pct drift. Literature: >5% suggests aerobic fatigue / poor pacing.

Acceptance:
- Activities with streams get a decoupling value populated.
- Activities without streams remain NULL (not zeroed).
- Test with synthetic streams that produce known decoupling value.
- 'coach status' could surface recent decoupling trend (follow-up).

Out of scope: real-time decoupling during rides (that's the watch's job).

title: [Phase 1 GAP] Wire push-week to bulk_upsert_tagged_events
description:
Plan's 'Commands split by determinism' lists 'coach push-week <week>' as deterministic. Current state: Phase 1 implementation is a DRY RUN only — prints the sessions_planned table for the given week_id and exits.

Why deferred in initial Phase 1 build: no sessions_planned rows exist until Phase 4 skills (bootstrap-plan, plan-training-week) populate them. Wiring the real call before source data existed would be dead code.

Now that the Phase 0 fork exposes bulk_upsert_tagged_events, this is a short bridge:

- Read sessions_planned WHERE week_id=<id>.
- Transform each row into the JSON shape bulk_upsert_tagged_events expects ({session_id, start_date_local, name, category, type, moving_time, distance, icu_training_load, description}).
- Plan_id comes from sessions_planned.plan_id (single plan per week assumed).
- Call the tool (via intervals MCP in CLI context — need to spawn subprocess OR call intervals ICUClient directly + replicate the upsert logic here).
- On success, update sessions_planned.pushed_to_intervals=1 and intervals_event_id with the returned event id.
- Emit events.jsonl line.

Simplest path: bypass MCP in the CLI and import the underlying upsert logic from tempo.intervals_upsert (or import the intervals_icu_mcp.tools.coach_extensions module and call the async function with a synthetic ctx). Avoids running the MCP server from the CLI.

Acceptance:
- 'coach push-week 2026-W17' after seeded sessions_planned writes events to intervals, sets pushed_to_intervals=1.
- Re-running is idempotent (external_id matches → update, not duplicate).
- Dry-run mode preserved via --dry-run flag.
- Phase 4 smoke test: bootstrap-plan → plan-training-week → push-week round-trips to intervals calendar.

Out of scope: conflict handling if user edits the event in intervals UI after push.

title: Phase 1: SQLite + sync scripts
description:
Foundation layer: disposable SQLite cache + deterministic sync/derive scripts + Typer CLI.

Scope (from plan: /home/seanm/.claude/plans/take-this-project-and-temporal-tarjan.md — Phase 1):
- scripts/db.py — 6-table schema (activities, wellness_daily, load_daily, sessions_planned, adherence, decisions), indexes, WAL mode, rebuildable.
- scripts/sync.py — pulls last N days from intervals → writes activities, wellness, raw JSONL audit trail.
- scripts/derive.py — CTL/ATL/TSB, decoupling, per-sport load.
- src/tempo/cli.py — Typer CLI: 'coach sync', 'coach status', 'coach push-week' (stub).

Milestone (verification):
1. 'coach sync' completes; sqlite3 data/coach.db "SELECT count(*) FROM activities" > 0.
2. 'coach status' prints CTL/ATL/TSB matching intervals.icu within 1%.
3. Delete coach.db, re-run sync, output identical (disposability proven).

Out of scope for Phase 1: LanceDB embeddings (Phase 2), coach-db MCP (Phase 3), skills/dashboards (Phase 4+), real push-week write (Phase 4).

Design invariants:
- coach.db is ALWAYS rebuildable from data/raw/ + plans/.
- Scripts are deterministic — no agentic reasoning in sync/derive.
- Append-only JSONL audit in data/raw/intervals/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl.

title: Add Phase 1 Python dependencies
description:
Add base deps for Phase 1 to pyproject.toml:

Required:
- typer (CLI)
- httpx (HTTP client; already transitively via intervals submodule)
- pydantic (shared schemas; already transitively)
- python-dateutil (date parsing)
- platformdirs (data dir resolution; optional — we have a fixed project-relative data/ path)

Recommended approach: add intervals-icu-mcp submodule as editable dependency so we reuse the tested ICUClient rather than re-rolling HTTP:

  [tool.uv.sources]
  intervals-icu-mcp = { path = "./mcp-servers/intervals-icu-mcp", editable = true }

  [project]
  dependencies = ["typer", "python-dateutil", "intervals-icu-mcp"]

This surfaces ICUClient, ActivitySummary, Wellness models. Tested in Phase 0 CI.

Dev deps (add via --dev): pytest, pytest-asyncio, respx, ruff, pyright. (Match submodule stack for consistency.)

Acceptance:
- 'uv sync' succeeds.
- 'uv run python -c "from intervals_icu_mcp.client import ICUClient"' succeeds from project root.
- ruff + pyright configs in pyproject (inherit submodule's as starting point).
close_reason: Landed in c86faf3. Phase 1 deps added: typer, rich, python-dateutil, python-dotenv, plus intervals-icu-mcp as editable path dep. Dev deps match submodule stack. Ruff + pyright configs inherit submodule conventions. Verified: uv sync succeeds, intervals_icu_mcp imports, coach stub runs, ruff + pyright clean.

title: SQLite schema module + migrations
description:
Implement the SQLite schema exactly as specified in the plan (Data Layer Details section).

File: src/tempo/db.py (module, importable). Exposes:
- connect(path: Path) -> sqlite3.Connection with WAL mode enabled, foreign_keys ON.
- init_schema(conn) -> None — idempotent DDL (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + indexes).
- SCHEMA_VERSION constant + simple _schema_migrations table for future evolution.
- DB_DEFAULT_PATH = Path('data/coach.db').

Tables (per plan):
- activities (PRIMARY KEY id TEXT; start_date, sport, duration_s, distance_m, tss, np, intensity_factor, avg_hr, max_hr, decoupling, elevation_gain_m, planned_session_id, plan_id, raw_json_path)
  - Indexes: idx_activities_start, idx_activities_sport_start
- wellness_daily (PK date; sleep_h, sleep_score, hrv, rhr, readiness, body_weight_kg, soreness, notes)
- load_daily (PK date; ctl, atl, tsb, ctl_bike, ctl_run, ctl_swim, ramp_7d)
- sessions_planned (PK id TEXT; plan_id, week_id, date, sport, library_ref, target_tss, target_duration_s, purpose, notes, pushed_to_intervals BOOL default 0, intervals_event_id)
  - Index: idx_sp_week
- adherence (PK planned_session_id REFERENCES sessions_planned(id); activity_id REFERENCES activities(id); completed, tss_delta, duration_delta_s, reason)
- decisions (PK id AUTOINCREMENT; timestamp, scope, kind, rationale, changed_files TEXT)
  - Index: idx_decisions_scope

Acceptance:
- init_schema against empty DB creates all 6 tables + indexes.
- Re-running init_schema on populated DB is a no-op (IF NOT EXISTS).
- Unit tests verify: PRAGMA journal_mode returns 'wal'; all tables present; FK enforcement on adherence.
- Path-resolver utility (tempo.paths module?) points at repo data/ dir via project root detection.

Out of scope: no INSERT helpers yet — those live in sync.py/derive.py.
close_reason: Landed. src/tempo/db.py with 6 tables + 4 indexes + _schema_migrations table. WAL mode enabled, FK enforcement on, idempotent init_schema(). 8 tests green.

title: Raw JSONL audit trail writer
description:
Append-only audit trail of every intervals API response. Per plan: 'This is what makes coach.db disposable.'

File: src/tempo/raw.py. Exposes:
- append_raw(source: str, payload: dict | list, now: datetime | None = None) -> Path
  - Writes one line of JSON (no embedded newlines) to data/raw/<source>/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl
  - Returns the file path written for storage in activities.raw_json_path.
  - Creates parent dirs as needed.
- Optional: weekly rotation — gzip files older than 7 days via 'coach raw compress' deferred utility. Can defer to Phase 7 polish — initial impl writes uncompressed jsonl only.

Line format: {"ts": ISO8601, "source": "intervals", "endpoint": "/activities", "params": {...}, "response": <full json>}

Acceptance:
- Appending two payloads same day → two lines in one file.
- Separate sources get separate subdirs (data/raw/intervals/, data/raw/strava/).
- Unit test with tmp dir.
- No reads of prior content (write-only, O(1)).
close_reason: Landed. src/tempo/raw.py append_raw() writes one JSON line per call into data/raw/<source>/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl. 7 tests green. Paths module (src/tempo/paths.py) added for shared resolution with TEMPO_DATA_DIR env override for test isolation.

title: Sync script: intervals → coach.db
description:
Deterministic pull from intervals.icu into coach.db, with JSONL audit trail.

File: src/tempo/sync.py. Not agentic — straight ETL.

Behaviour:
1. Load ICUConfig from env (reuse intervals_icu_mcp.auth.load_config).
2. Open async ICUClient.
3. Pull activities for window [today-days .. today]; default days=90; --full sets days=365*3 for initial backfill.
4. Pull wellness same window.
5. For each response: append_raw(...) → then upsert into SQLite.
6. Upsert keyed on primary key (activity.id, wellness.date). Idempotent — re-running does not duplicate rows.
7. For activities: map ActivitySummary fields → activities table columns. Unmapped fields stay in raw_json_path dump only.
8. Write data/events.jsonl line: {ts, command='sync', days, activities_new, activities_updated, wellness_new, wellness_updated, duration_ms}.

Sport mapping: intervals 'Ride'/'Run'/'Swim'/'WeightTraining' → coach.db sport bike/run/swim/strength. Unknown sports get 'other'.

CLI wiring: called by 'coach sync [--days N] [--full]' in the next ticket.

Acceptance:
- First run on empty DB populates activities + wellness; raw_json_path populated and file exists.
- Second run produces zero inserts, zero updates if nothing changed upstream (or only updates changed rows).
- Unit tests with respx mocks mirroring submodule's test pattern.
- 'sqlite3 data/coach.db "SELECT count(*) FROM activities"' > 0 post-sync (one of three milestone criteria).

Out of scope: deriving CTL/ATL/TSB — that's derive.py. Sync writes raw+normalized activities/wellness only.
close_reason: Landed. sync.py pulls activities + wellness for N-day window, appends every response to data/raw/intervals/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl, upserts normalized rows into coach.db. Idempotent (INSERT...ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE). Sport types mapped to bike/run/swim/strength/other. Emits events.jsonl line per run. 4 tests green with respx mocks.

title: Derive script: CTL/ATL/TSB + decoupling
description:
Deterministic derivations from activities → load_daily and per-activity decoupling column.

File: src/tempo/derive.py. Not agentic.

Derivations (per plan Data Layer Details):

1. Per-day training load rollup:
   - Sum activities.tss per date → day_tss.
   - CTL (chronic training load) = 42-day exponentially-weighted average. Standard formula: CTL_today = CTL_yesterday + (day_tss - CTL_yesterday)/42.
   - ATL (acute) = 7-day EWA. ATL_today = ATL_yesterday + (day_tss - ATL_yesterday)/7.
   - TSB = CTL - ATL.
   - Per-sport CTL (ctl_bike, ctl_run, ctl_swim) using the same formula restricted to activities of that sport.
   - ramp_7d = CTL[today] - CTL[today-7d].

2. Decoupling (aerobic decoupling = Pw:HR drift):
   - For each activity with streams available: Pw:HR first-half vs second-half ratio.
   - Phase 1 can use a simpler proxy if streams not synced: skip (leave NULL) and ticket Phase 2/3 for full implementation once streams land.
   - Acceptance: decoupling column nullable; populated only when streams present in raw payload.

3. Upsert rows into load_daily for every date in [first_activity, today]. Backfill gaps with zero load.

4. Write events.jsonl line: {ts, command='derive', days_updated, activities_scored, duration_ms}.

Initial seed: CTL_0/ATL_0 = 0 at first-activity date - 42d. Warm-up effect cleared after ~42 days of history.

Validation target (milestone): load_daily.ctl/atl/tsb for today must match intervals.icu athlete endpoint values within 1% once Phase 0 creds exist.

Acceptance:
- Unit test with synthetic TSS series — closed-form check CTL after 42 days at constant tss=100 approaches 100 (within 0.5 TSS).
- Per-sport CTL sums approximately equal aggregate CTL (small drift acceptable from rounding).
- Idempotent — re-running produces identical load_daily rows.

Out of scope: decoupling via streams if not easily available in ActivitySummary. Ticket follow-up if needed.
close_reason: Landed. derive.py computes CTL (42d EWA), ATL (7d EWA), TSB, per-sport CTL (bike/run/swim), and 7-day ramp rate. Walks first_activity - 42d to today for EWA burn-in. Idempotent via DELETE + re-INSERT. Decoupling left NULL per ticket scope (needs streams, not in ActivitySummary). 7 tests green including EWA convergence at constant load.

title: Typer CLI: coach sync/status/push-week
description:
Wire deterministic Phase 1 verbs into the existing 'coach' Typer CLI entrypoint.

File: src/tempo/cli.py (replace Phase 0 stub). Keep main() as Typer app object with registered subcommands.

Commands:

1. coach sync [--days=90] [--full]
   - Runs sync.py (fetch activities + wellness, write raw JSONL, upsert DB).
   - Then runs derive.py (recompute load_daily).
   - Both steps emit progress (Rich/stderr OK). Returns nonzero on any failure.

2. coach status
   - Reads from coach.db only. No network.
   - Prints:
     - Today's CTL/ATL/TSB (from load_daily where date=today or latest).
     - Ramp rate (7d).
     - Current week: planned sessions count (from sessions_planned WHERE week_id=current), completed count (from adherence joined).
     - Latest wellness: hrv, rhr, sleep_h, readiness (one-row summary from wellness_daily).
     - Current active injury flags (read athlete/injury-log.md 'Active' section — simple regex parse OK).
   - Output format: structured text with section headers; Rich Table if Rich is a dep.

3. coach push-week <week-id>
   - Phase 1 STUB: reads sessions_planned WHERE week_id=<id>, prints a dry-run table. Does NOT call intervals MCP.
   - Real implementation lands in Phase 4 alongside skills. Ticket a Phase 4 issue when bootstrap-plan is built.

Out of scope for Phase 1: vectors rebuild (Phase 2), research/ingest/plan/review verbs (Phase 4+).

Acceptance:
- 'coach --help' lists sync, status, push-week.
- 'coach sync --days=7' against live API (post-creds) produces rows; subsequent status shows CTL/ATL/TSB.
- All three milestone verifications pass (see tempo-wns.8).
close_reason: Landed. Typer CLI with 3 verbs: sync (calls sync + derive), status (CTL/ATL/TSB + week progress + wellness + injury flags), push-week (Phase 1 dry-run only — real push is Phase 4). 6 CliRunner tests green.

title: Phase 1 test suite + CI gates
description:
Harness for Phase 1 code quality.

Scope:
- tests/ directory in project root with pytest-asyncio config mirroring submodule's conftest style.
- Unit tests per module: test_db.py, test_raw.py, test_sync.py (respx-mocked), test_derive.py (synthetic TSS), test_cli.py (Typer CliRunner).
- Integration test 'test_disposability.py': populate DB from fixtures, snapshot load_daily, delete DB, re-run sync+derive from same raw JSONL (offline mode), assert identical load_daily — this is milestone criterion #3 (disposability).
- pyproject.toml: ruff config (inherit submodule's line-length, rules), pyright config (strict mode on src/tempo/, lenient on tests/).
- Makefile target 'make can-release' equivalent: 'uv run ruff check && uv run pyright && uv run pytest'.

Acceptance:
- 'uv run pytest' — all green.
- 'uv run ruff check' — clean.
- 'uv run pyright' — clean on src/tempo/.
- Coverage target: >80% on src/tempo/ (not enforced hard; informational).

Out of scope: snapshot fixtures of real intervals payloads — use synthetic/mocked for Phase 1 unit tests.
close_reason: Landed. Disposability integration test (test_disposability.py) — milestone criterion #3 verified in CI. Top-level Makefile with install/sync/status/test/lint/format/can-release targets. Full suite: 33 tests green (db, raw, sync, derive, cli, disposability). make can-release green (ruff + pyright + pytest).

title: Phase 1 milestone verification
description:
End-to-end verify Phase 1 against the plan's three stated criteria. Requires live intervals creds (tempo-b7z) and real Phase 1 code landed.

Verification steps (from plan's Verification section, Phase 1):

1. 'coach sync' completes without error.
   - sqlite3 data/coach.db 'SELECT count(*) FROM activities' → > 0.
   - sqlite3 data/coach.db 'SELECT count(*) FROM wellness_daily' → > 0 (if wellness history exists).
   - ls data/raw/intervals/*.jsonl — at least one file.

2. 'coach status' prints CTL/ATL/TSB.
   - Cross-check against intervals.icu athlete endpoint (via MCP: get_fitness_summary).
   - Values must match within 1% (absolute CTL diff, not relative).
   - Log the diff to the ticket notes.

3. Disposability proof:
   - Backup: cp data/coach.db /tmp/coach-pre.db
   - rm data/coach.db
   - coach sync --from-raw (or simply 'coach sync' — re-fetches; then also test the offline re-derive path using only data/raw/).
   - Diff load_daily row-by-row against /tmp/coach-pre.db snapshot.
   - Identical within floating-point epsilon.

Close reason must include: activity count, CTL diff vs intervals, disposability diff result.

Blockers: tempo-b7z (credentials). This ticket is inert until that lands.

This closes the Phase 1 epic (tempo-wns).

title: [P0] Seed athlete/injury-log.md
description: Running log template for active and resolved niggles. Active-flag entry format (status, onset, symptoms, constraints, rehab, next-review). Decision rule R-5 makes this the highest-priority input to weekly planning.
close_reason: athlete/injury-log.md written. Active section empty ('no active flags'), commented template for new entries (status/onset/symptoms/constraints/rehab/check-in), resolved archive section. Referenced by decision rule R-5 (HARD) as highest-priority planning input.
